depth_guidance.deep: Include more nuance about how the engagement pattern shifts across the subject corpus.
depth_guidance.standard: Assign a level plus divergence description and evidence anchors.
depth_guidance.surface: Assign a level and brief rationale for each theme.

analytical_dimensions.description: Determine the engagement level for each theme.
probing_questions: Does the subject author address this theme directly?
Is the engagement partial, ignored, or distorted?

depth_guidance.deep: Show the pattern across the subject corpus with more granular evidence.
depth_guidance.standard: Explain the divergence and severity theme by theme.

analytical_dimensions.description: Describe how the subject author diverges from the source thinker within each theme.
probing_questions: Is the divergence substantive, framing-based, emphatic, or omissive?

depth_scaling.deep: Richer patterning across works and stronger evidential justification.
depth_scaling.standard: Engagement label, divergence description, and severity.

capabilities.description: Produce a stable engagement map keyed by the source themes.

consumes_from.themes: Requires the source thinker's thematic synthesis and theme identifiers.

shares_with.engagements: Theme-keyed engagement objects that later AOI findings can inherit.

depth_levels.description: Minimal engagement labeling for each theme.
depth_levels.suitable_for: Fast triage of the subject author's relationship to the source themes.

depth_levels.description: Structured engagement mapping with divergence and severity.
depth_levels.suitable_for: Default AOI engagement map for the single-thinker tranche.

depth_levels.description: Expanded engagement mapping with fuller evidential support and cross-text nuance.
depth_levels.suitable_for: Dense subject corpora where theme treatment varies substantially by text.

key_concepts.definition: A bounded assessment of whether a theme is engaged, partial, ignored, or distorted.
key_concepts.name: engagement_level

primary.description: Skinner's contextual method helps distinguish genuine engagement from projection or omission.
primary.name: skinner

secondary.description: Bloom's agonistic account of influence frames engagement as selective, strategic, and often conflictual.
secondary.name: bloom

traditions.description: Engagement is tracked through how a source is cited, appropriated, or omitted.
traditions.name: citation_analysis

engagements.theme_id: string - must match an upstream theme identifier

problematique:
Once the source thinker's thematic map exists, the next question is how the
subject author engages those themes. Engagement is not binary. The subject can
genuinely grapple with a theme, touch it only partially, ignore it despite its
relevance, or engage it in a distorted way that already signals a later AOI
problem.

This engine therefore maps the subject author's engagement theme by theme,
keeping the selected source thinker fixed while reading the subject corpus as a
response field. The output is a structured engagement map keyed by theme_id so
later AOI findings can inherit stable thematic coordinates.
researcher_question: How does the subject author engage each theme from the selected source thinker?
